scenario: Pinnacle Marketplace runs Camunda 8.8 and an operator is repairing a process instance that handles 1500 orders per hour, where a pricing variable on the root scope was set to the wrong currency 2 hours ago. The retailer wants to correct the existing variable value on that root scope without cancelling any tokens, creating new scopes, or restarting the process instance.
question: Which Operate workflow queues an Edit Variable modification on the existing scope without creating any new scope?

options.text: Click Add Variable in the variables panel on the existing scope, type a new name for the corrected currency, and the value change is queued as a pending Edit Variable on that root scope
Select the existing scope in the instance history, change the value in the variables panel, blur out of the field, and the Edit Variable modification is queued on that existing scope
Open the diagram, right-click the flow node that owns the variable, choose Edit Variable from its dropdown, and the corrected value is queued as a pending Edit Variable on that scope
Switch to the modification summary modal, choose the JSON Diff Viewer for the variable, type the new value inline, and the Edit Variable modification is queued on the existing root scope

a.text: Incorrect. Add Variable creates a brand new variable on the scope. Correcting an existing value uses Edit Variable, which is driven by changing the value in the variables panel of the selected scope, not by Add Variable.

b.text: Correct. The docs state that during modification mode, selecting an existing scope in the instance history, changing the value in the variables panel, and blurring out with a valid value queues an Edit Variable pending modification on that scope.

c.text: Incorrect. Edit Variable is not driven from a flow node dropdown on the diagram. The value change happens in the variables panel of the selected existing scope in the instance history.

d.text: Incorrect. The summary modal only displays pending modifications and offers a JSON Diff Viewer for review. The edit itself is performed earlier in the variables panel by changing the value and blurring out of the field.

explanation: Editing an existing variable in modification mode is done from the variables panel of the selected scope. The operator picks the existing scope in the instance history, changes the value, and on blur with a valid value the Edit Variable modification is queued as pending.
